critical.mandate: Use sprint state machine: backlog, validating, ready-for-dev, in-progress, invalid, review, done
Only stories with status 'ready-for-dev' are selectable for a sprint

step.title: Load Epics, Stories, Action Items, and Previous Retro
step.action: Read {implementation_artifacts}/action-items.yaml if it exists. Check for HIGH priority items open for 2+ sprints (escalation_count >= 2 AND priority == "high" AND status == "open"). If any found: HALT — "Cannot start sprint planning. {count} HIGH priority action item(s) have been open for {escalation_count}+ sprints: {for each: A-{NNN}: {title} (open since {source_sprint}) → Run: /gaia action A-{NNN}} Resolve each item using the command shown above before re-running sprint planning." For MEDIUM/LOW open items: display warning but do NOT halt: "Note: {count} action items still open (non-blocking)."
Read {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md
Parse all stories with their priorities, sizes, dependencies
Scan {implementation_artifacts}/ for individual story files matching {story_key}-*.md. For each file found, read its frontmatter Status field.

Classify stories into selectable and non-selectable: - SELECTABLE: stories with individual files AND status = 'ready-for-dev' - NOT SELECTABLE: stories without individual files → "Story {key} has no individual file — run /gaia-create-story {key} first." - NOT SELECTABLE: stories with files but status ≠ 'ready-for-dev' → "Story {key} is in '{status}' status — must be 'ready-for-dev' to be selectable. Run /gaia-validate-story {key} first."
Display the classification to the user: selectable stories table (Key | Title | Priority | Size | Risk | Status) and non-selectable stories with reasons.
Load most recent retro-{sprint_id}.md from {implementation_artifacts}/ if available
If retro found: extract open action items — carry forward as sprint constraints or tasks. Present to user: "Previous retro action items to carry forward:" with status of each.

step.title: Optional: Memory Hygiene Check
step.action: Check if any sidecar directories under {memory_path}/*-sidecar/ contain content beyond their template header

check.ask: Agent memory sidecars contain decisions from previous sessions. Would you like to run memory hygiene? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended if architecture or requirement changes occurred since last sprint. (yes / skip)
check.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/anytime/memory-hygiene/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: memory hygiene can be run anytime with /gaia-memory-hygiene

check.ask: No agent memory sidecar data found. Would you like to run memory hygiene anyway? This is typically skipped for first sprints. (yes / skip)
check.action: If yes: spawn subagent as above
If skip: continue to next step

step.title: Story Selection
step.action: Read sizing_map from {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml. Use these canonical point values for T-shirt sizes (S/M/L/XL → points). Display the mapping to the user before selection.
Select stories for this sprint based on priority and dependencies — only from stories classified as SELECTABLE in Step 1 (status = 'ready-for-dev').
Ensure total size fits within velocity estimate using the sizing_map point values

Dependency blocking: for each candidate story, check its depends_on list. If any dependency story is NOT in 'done' status, the story CANNOT be included in this sprint. Display: "BLOCKED: Story {key} depends on {dep_key} (status: {dep_status}) — cannot be included until {dep_key} is done."

Priority surfacing: after story selection, check for P0 stories that are 'ready-for-dev' but were NOT selected. If any found, display: "WARNING: The following P0/critical stories are ready but not selected for this sprint:" followed by the list with their sizes. Ask user to confirm they intentionally excluded these high-priority stories.
If {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md exists: apply risk levels to story selection — buffer 20% for high-risk stories in velocity estimate

ATDD check (high-risk ONLY): for each story with risk = high, check if {test_artifacts}/atdd-{story_key}.md exists. If missing, add a sprint risk note: "HIGH-RISK story {key} has no ATDD file — run /gaia-atdd {key} before development." Do NOT flag medium or low risk stories — ATDD is required only for high-risk stories.

step.title: Update Story Files
step.action: For each selected story that has an individual file in {implementation_artifacts}/: update the sprint_id field to "sprint-{N}"

For each selected story: invoke status-sync protocol to assign the sprint — keep status as 'ready-for-dev' (do NOT change to backlog). Stories remain ready-for-dev until /gaia-dev-story transitions them to in-progress.
Note: sprint-status.yaml may not exist yet at this point (created in Step 8) — the protocol will skip the sprint-status.yaml update if the file doesn't exist.
Include all modified story files in the checkpoint files_touched with sha256 checksums

step.title: Optional: Mobile Testing
step.ask: Would you like to create a mobile test plan? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Recommended for mobile or responsive applications. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/testing/workflows/mobile-testing/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: mobile testing can be set up anytime later with /gaia-mobile-testing

step.title: Sprint Plan Generation
step.action: Create sprint plan with story assignments and execution order
Order by dependency resolution + priority

Generate a Sprint Burndown Estimate table showing projected points remaining per day based on wave/story completion schedule. Format: Day | Points Remaining | Stories Completing. This helps the team track expected progress and identify deviations early.

step.title: Status Initialization
step.action: All selected stories remain in 'ready-for-dev' status (assigned to sprint but not yet started)

Generate sprint-status.yaml with standardized schema: sprint_id: "sprint-{N}" duration: "{1 week / 2 weeks / custom}" velocity_capacity: {velocity estimate from Step 3} team_size: {number of developers} total_points: {sum of all selected story points} capacity_utilization: "{total_points / velocity_capacity * 100}%" started: "{date}" end_date: "{date}" stories: - key: "{story-key}" title: "{story title}" status: "ready-for-dev" points: {story points from sizing_map} risk_level: "{high/medium/low}" assignee: null blocked_by: null updated: "{date}"

step.title: Generate Output
step.template-output: Generate sprint plan document with: sprint goals, selected stories (ordered), velocity target, risk assessment, Sprint Burndown Estimate (points remaining per day), and a Testing Readiness section listing ONLY high-risk stories with their ATDD file status. Medium and low risk stories do NOT require ATDD and should NOT appear in the ATDD readiness checks.
step.action: Write sprint-status.yaml to {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml

step.title: Val Validation
step.action: Invoke Val to validate the sprint plan. Val verifies: (a) All selected story keys exist as story files with status 'ready-for-dev' (b) Dependency ordering is correct — no story selected before its blocking dependency is 'done' (c) Points math is correct — total selected points ≤ velocity capacity (d) No duplicate story keys in the selection (e) Sprint-status.yaml entries match the sprint plan document If findings: SM auto-fixes (e.g., remove invalid stories, fix ordering), re-validates. If Val fails or crashes: log warning and continue — validation is non-blocking for sprint planning.

step.title: Save to Val Memory
step.action:
Auto-save sprint planning decisions to Val's memory sidecar (no user prompt required):
1. Append to {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/decision-log.md: ### [YYYY-MM-DD] Sprint Planning: {sprint_id}
- **Agent:** validator - **Workflow:** sprint-planning - **Sprint:** {sprint_id} - **Status:** recorded
Sprint {sprint_id} planned. Duration: {duration}. Velocity: {velocity_capacity} pts. Stories selected: {count} ({total_points} pts, {utilization}% capacity). Story keys: {comma-separated list}. High-risk stories: {count} (ATDD: {verified/missing}). Retro action items carried forward: {count or "none"}.
2. Replace body of {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/conversation-context.md (preserve header above first ---): Last session: Sprint planning for {sprint_id}. Date: {YYYY-MM-DD}. Stories: {count} ({total_points} pts). Velocity: {velocity_capacity}.
If {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ directory or files don't exist, create them with standard headers. If writing fails, log warning and continue — memory save is non-blocking.

next-step.primary: Start implementing the first story from the sprint
